_mode_3_notes: The bolt-4 SCM scanner does NOT invoke Mode-1 Ivy resolution.
It falls through to Mode-3 default path scan: SHA1 fingerprints of binary files (jar/war/dll/etc).
Each lib/*.jar appears as a flat direct dependency with no transitive structure.
Transitive-control semantics are exercised at lib-population time: we committed exactly the JARs that Ivy WOULD have produced post-resolution.
The ABSENCE of commons-logging-*.jar and commons-collections-*.jar in lib/ is the regression signal.
If Mend reports those JARs, it means it scanned a different source (e.g. a local Maven/Ivy cache) rather than only lib/.
Mode-1 testing would require pipeline-mode `mend ua -c whitesource.config`, not SCM integration.

mechanism_1_plain_transitive.note: failureaccess is the regression signal: must appear (guava's transitive resolved normally)

mechanism_2_transitive_false.note: commons-collections-*.jar absence is the regression signal for transitive=false

mechanism_3_exclude.note: commons-logging-*.jar absence is the regression signal for <exclude>

guava-32.1.3-jre.jar._note: Direct dep — mechanism 1 (plain transitive): its transitives failureaccess and listenablefuture are also in lib/

failureaccess-1.0.1.jar._note: Guava transitive — should appear (mechanism 1 regression signal: catalog #13)

listenablefuture-9999.0-empty-to-avoid-c._note: Guava transitive — empty JAR placeholder; appears in lib/ because transitive=true (default)

commons-beanutils-1.8.3.jar._note: Direct dep with transitive=false (mechanism 2). Its children (commons-logging:1.1.1, commons-collections:3.2.2) must NOT appear in lib/.

httpclient-4.5.14.jar._note: Direct dep with <exclude> on commons-logging (mechanism 3). httpcore and commons-codec kept.

httpcore-4.4.16.jar._note: httpclient transitive — kept after <exclude> on commons-logging

commons-codec-1.11.jar._note: httpclient transitive — kept after <exclude> on commons-logging

warnings: REGRESSION SIGNAL: The absence of commons-logging-*.jar in lib/ exercises BOTH mechanism 2 (transitive=false on beanutils, which would have brought commons-logging:1.1.1) and mechanism 3 (<exclude> on httpclient, which would have brought commons-logging:1.2). If Mend reports any commons-logging JAR, it scanned a source outside lib/ (e.g. a cached Ivy/Maven repo on the agent host). Catalog IDs: #9 (exclude), #17 (transitive-false).

REGRESSION SIGNAL: The absence of commons-collections-*.jar in lib/ exercises mechanism 2 (transitive=false on commons-beanutils:1.8.3). Its POM lists commons-collections:3.2.2 as a dependency; with transitive=false, Ivy skips it. Catalog ID: #17.

Mode-3 pivot: The bolt-4 SCM scanner does not honor ant.ivyResolveDependencies=true from whitesource.config. JARs were pre-resolved and committed to lib/ per Mode-3 requirements. The ivy.xml and ivysettings.xml are present for documentation and pipeline-mode mend-ua use only.

listenablefuture-9999.0-empty-to-avoid-conflict-with-guava.jar is a real Maven Central artifact (an empty JAR, 2199 bytes). Mend should fingerprint it and assign a known SHA1. If Mend skips it due to the unusual version string, that is a secondary detection signal.
Partial reproducibility limitation: Ant version is not pinnable via install-tool. Only java is pinned (17). Downstream comparators should treat the Ant binary version as uncontrolled.
